profile.name: Rober
profile.role_in_household: Primary; tends to lead financial discussions and wants a clear retirement plan while keeping the household stable with three kids.
profile.financial_literacy_level: High—comfortable with account types, interest rates, and tradeoffs; often sanity-checks figures and asks for assumptions.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Conservative in behavior: prioritizes principal protection and predictable outcomes; asks about downside scenarios before agreeing to changes.
profile.communication_style: Structured and detail-first; will quote numbers he remembers, correct small inaccuracies, and use hedges like "if I’m remembering right"; pauses to do quick math.
emotional_tendencies: calmer with checklists and written next steps
profile.decision_making_style: Wants 2–3 options compared side-by-side, then decides after a brief cooling-off period; prefers rules-based plans and documentation.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Cautiously open—trust rises with precision, transparency, and no sales pressure.
hidden_concerns: worries about health being only "fair" and potential long-term care costs
concerned about being responsible if the plan underperforms

profile.name: Susa
profile.role_in_household: Spouse/partner; focuses on day-to-day logistics and whether decisions improve monthly breathing room and reduce stress.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate—good with budgeting and basics, but prefers plain English and round numbers; occasionally mixes up terms (gross vs net, premium vs benefit, balance vs payment).
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Cautious but simplicity-driven: supports conservative investing if it feels "set-and-forget"; open to small changes that reduce complexity or stabilize cash flow.
profile.communication_style: Warm and practical; speaks in ranges ("around," "roughly"), asks clarifying questions, and may interrupt to translate decisions into monthly impact; uses fillers like "um" and "I think."
profile.decision_making_style: Consensus-driven; wants Robert confident in the numbers but needs the plan to feel livable month-to-month; prefers a short recommendation list.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Generally trusting if listened to and not rushed; trust drops if she feels talked down to.
internal_biases: status quo bias (prefers fewer moving parts at once)
hidden_concerns: worries about staying on track with three kids and a big monthly expense load
concerned about tying up cash and then needing it unexpectedly
